steps.title: Rearrange to reveal the critical value
steps.content:
Starting with $x(r - 7) + 3 = 19x + 25$, collect $x$ terms on one side:
$$xr - 7x - 19x = 25 - 3$$
$$x(r - 26) = 22$$

steps.title: Identify when there is exactly one solution
steps.content:
A linear equation $ax = b$ has exactly one solution when $a eq 0$. Here, the coefficient is $(r - 26)$, so we need $r - 26 eq 0$, which means $r eq 26$.

Test each choice:
  • $r = 3$: coefficient is $-23$ → $x = rac{22}{-23}$ (one solution) ✓
  • $r = 7$: coefficient is $-19$ → $x = rac{22}{-19}$ (one solution) ✓
  • $r = 22$: coefficient is $-4$ → $x = rac{22}{-4}$ (one solution) ✓
  • $r = 26$: coefficient is $0$ → equation becomes $0 = 22$ (no solution) ✗

Therefore $r$ cannot be 26. That's choice D — D is correct.
